warnings.noApiKey: No OpenRouter API key set. Open settings to add one.

workspace.title: Workspace folder
workspace.hint: Your project folders are created and stored here.

models.title: Default models
models.hint: Pre-selected for new projects (changeable per project).
models.videoPlaceholder: e.g. google/veo-3.1 — can also be set per scene

imageProvider.title: Image generation
imageProvider.notRunning: ComfyUI not reachable — start it with its API on port 8188.
imageProvider.comfyHint: Tip: a 'Hyper' or 'Schnell' model with few steps keeps the Mac responsive while generating.

projects.title: Projects
projects.newPlaceholder: Name of the new film project…
projects.emptyNoWorkspace: Please pick a workspace folder in settings first.
projects.empty: No projects yet. Create your first one above.

idea.title: Your film idea
idea.hint: Describe your scene, idea or story. A storyboard with camera work and cuts is planned from it.
idea.placeholder: e.g. A lone astronaut discovers the ruins of an ancient civilization on an alien planet…

refine.placeholder: e.g. "Make scene 3 more dramatic" or "Add a chase after scene 2"

modelSelect.noMatch: No match — press Enter for custom entry

editor.confirmGenerateAll: Auto-fill all scenes with a storyboard image and video job? (Storyboards are auto-approved)
editor.snapTitle: Adjust all scene durations to the nearest valid value for this model
editor.charPlaceholder: e.g. Main character: muscular man in his mid-30s, dark hair, traditional vest, always the same
editor.negPlaceholder: character transformation, gender change, species change, morphing, inconsistent character…
editor.negEmpty: leave empty = default value is used
editor.priceHint: Click a row to select the model. E.g. 6s/scene × 8 scenes ≈ $3–14 per run with Veo 3.1 Fast.
editor.selectVideoFirst: Please select a video model first.
editor.allStarted: ✓ All scenes started — videos finish in the background.
editor.jobsFailed_one: ⚠️ {{count}} video job failed — click to retry.
editor.jobsFailed_other: ⚠️ {{count}} video jobs failed — click to retry.
editor.exportReadyHint: All videos are ready. Click the button to stitch them into a film with ffmpeg.
editor.exportWaitHint: Once all videos are generated they get stitched here. Pending videos are auto-approved.

transitions.label: Transition to next scene
transitions.needsVideoFirst: Generate this scene's video and the next storyboard first.
transitions.morphHint: A separate bridge clip of its own length (does not shorten the scenes). It flows from this scene's end into the next scene's first image. Needs a video model with end-frame support.

audioToggle.label: Sound (per scene)
audioToggle.hint: Sound is generated per scene with the video. Only audio-capable video models are shown.

music.title: 🎵 Music (whole film)
music.hint: One continuous track laid over the finished film — no cuts at scene boundaries. Mixed under the per-scene sound (or louder if there is none).

scene.selectVideoModel: Please select a video model first.

cast.emptyProject: No entries yet. Create characters or entities that should stay consistent across all scenes.
cast.globalHint: Characters/entities you use across projects. Click "Import" to copy into the current project.
cast.globalEmpty: Empty. Push a project entry into the global library with ⭐.
cast.name: Name
cast.styleNotesPlaceholder: Behavior, instrument, dance style, typical pose…
cast.visionHint: Needs a vision-capable model (e.g. Claude, GPT-4o)
cast.photoHint: 📌 = set directly as reference image (no AI step needed). Even without a reference image the first photo is used automatically as a visual template.
cast.refImageHint: Uses photos + description as a base. This image is used as a visual anchor in storyboards.

items.a: A self-hosted studio that turns an idea into a short film: it plans scenes, generates a storyboard image per scene, then generates a video clip per scene and stitches them into one film — all via AI models on OpenRouter.

items.q: How do I create my first film?
items.a: 1) Add your OpenRouter API key in Settings. 2) Pick a workspace folder. 3) Create a project, type an idea, and let it plan the scenes. 4) Generate storyboards, then videos, then export.

items.q: Where do I get an API key?
items.a: Create one at openrouter.ai under Keys. Paste it in Settings → OpenRouter API key. It is stored locally on your machine and never written into project files.
Cost depends on the chosen models and total video seconds. The cost bar at the bottom of a project shows what has been spent and an estimate for generating all remaining scenes before you commit.

items.q: Why must I pick the video model first?
items.a: Each video model supports only certain clip durations. Picking it first lets the app snap every scene to a valid length, so generation does not fail later.

items.q: Which model generates the storyboard images?
items.a: Only true image-output models work (e.g. Gemini Image, GPT Image). Multimodal chat models that merely read images cannot create them and are filtered out of the image dropdown.

items.q: How do I keep a character looking the same?
items.a: Create the character under Cast & Entities, add reference photos or generate a reference image, and it gets injected into every scene prompt. A negative prompt prevents transformations.

items.q: How is the final film made?
items.a: Approved clips are concatenated with ffmpeg in scene order. Make sure ffmpeg is installed (e.g. brew install ffmpeg).
The scene card shows the error message. Common causes: an invalid model id or a model that cannot output images. Fix the model in the model bar and retry the scene.
